g.text: What is Big Data Analytics?

g.data-narration: Definition of Big Data Analytics
g.text: Big Data Analytics is the process of examining data sets that are very large and complex, in order to extract insights and knowledge.

g.data-narration: Evolution of Big Data Analytics
g.text: The evolution of Big Data Analytics can be described as follows:

g.data-narration: 1950s-1980s: Data analysis was a manual process that involved looking at small to moderate-sized datasets.
g.text: 1950s-1980s: Data analysis was a manual process that involved looking at small to moderate-sized datasets.

g.data-narration: 1990s-2000s: Data analysis became more automated with the introduction of statistical packages and data visualization tools.
g.text: 1990s-2000s: Data analysis became more automated with the introduction of statistical packages and data visualization tools.

g.data-narration: 2010s: The rise of social media, mobile devices, and the Internet of Things (IoT) led to an explosion in data generation.
g.text: 2010s: The rise of social media, mobile devices, and the Internet of Things (IoT) led to an explosion in data generation.

g.data-narration: Present Day: Big Data Analytics is a global phenomenon that involves analyzing large amounts of complex data from multiple sources.
g.text: Present Day: Big Data Analytics is a global phenomenon that involves analyzing large amounts of complex data from multiple sources.

g.data-narration: Characteristics of Big Data Analytics
g.text: Characteristics of Big Data Analytics:

g.data-narration: Volume: Large amounts of data.
g.text: Volume: Large amounts of data.

g.data-narration: Velocity: Data is generated rapidly.
g.text: Velocity: Data is generated rapidly.

g.data-narration: Variety: Data comes from multiple sources.
g.text: Variety: Data comes from multiple sources.

g.data-narration: Veracity: Data may be noisy or inaccurate.
g.text: Veracity: Data may be noisy or inaccurate.

g.data-narration: Value: Insights and knowledge can be extracted from the data.
g.text: Value: Insights and knowledge can be extracted from the data.

g.data-narration: Example of Big Data Analytics
g.text: Example of Big Data Analytics:

g.data-narration: Example 1: A retailer uses Big Data Analytics to analyze customer purchase behavior, find trends, and optimize marketing strategies.
g.text: Example 1: A retailer uses Big Data Analytics to analyze customer purchase behavior, find trends, and optimize marketing strategies.

g.data-narration: Example 2: A healthcare organization uses Big Data Analytics to analyze medical records, identify patterns, and improve patient outcomes.
g.text: Example 2: A healthcare organization uses Big Data Analytics to analyze medical records, identify patterns, and improve patient outcomes.

g.data-narration: Classification of Analytics
g.text: Classification of Analytics:

g.data-narration: Descriptive Analytics: Involves analyzing historical data to understand what happened.
g.text: Descriptive Analytics: Involves analyzing historical data to understand what happened.

g.data-narration: Diagnostic Analytics: Involves analyzing data to identify trends and patterns.
g.text: Diagnostic Analytics: Involves analyzing data to identify trends and patterns.

g.data-narration: Predictive Analytics: Involves analyzing data to predict future events.
g.text: Predictive Analytics: Involves analyzing data to predict future events.
